nodes.label: httpapi.asp HTTPS Transport (self-signed + mTLS)
nodes.rationale: HTTPS on 443 with a self-signed cert (CN www.linkplay.com) requiring TLS verification off; some firmware (WiiM Ultra fw 5.x) requires the shared LinkPlay mTLS client cert embedded in linkplay-cert.ts.

nodes.label: getPlayerStatusEx (playback status)
nodes.rationale: Title/Artist/Album are hex-encoded UTF-8 and may contain HTML entities; left empty for Bluetooth, where metadata comes from getMetaInfo (AVRCP) instead.

nodes.label: getMetaInfo → metaData
nodes.rationale: No codec or vendor field exists anywhere in the WiiM API; the device reports decoded PCM bitDepth even for lossy streams, so bitRate is the reliable lossy-vs-lossless signal.

nodes.label: setPlayerCmd:playlist Index-Ignored Caveat
nodes.rationale: On WiiM Ultra fw 5.x the <index> is ignored: playback starts at a stale internal playlist cursor that cannot be reset by stop, playindex, switchindex, a .m3u extension, or unique content — which is why NAS/DLNA playback uses UPnP AVTransport instead.

nodes.label: Asymmetric Loop/Shuffle Read-vs-Write Tables
nodes.rationale: The read field `loop` and the write command `loopmode` use different tables on current firmware; the older python-linkplay/Home-Assistant enum mis-maps modes (e.g. sets Repeat-One when asked for Repeat-All).

nodes.label: plm_support Input Bitmask
nodes.rationale: Doesn't reliably flag every input (Ultra USB, HDMI ARC), so some sources are offered explicitly and the currently-playing source is always kept selectable.

nodes.label: mode → Streaming Service Mapping
nodes.rationale: There is no vendor field, so Connect/cast sessions map from dedicated mode codes and generic in-app streaming (mode 10/20) is guessed by sniffing the albumArtURI host.

nodes.label: Per-source LV2 EQ Commands (Eq10HP / EqNp)
nodes.rationale: Community-verified (WiiM Ultra fw5.2+); the EQ card self-disables if the device stops answering these commands (firmware kill-switch).

nodes.label: Presets (getPresetInfo / MCUKeyShortClick)
nodes.rationale: Preset slots are read-only over HTTP: they can be listed and played but not set or reordered from the API.

nodes.label: No Native Favorite/Like Command
nodes.rationale: Confirmed against the official PDF, python-linkplay and the community OpenAPI; the WiiM app's heart calls each streaming service's own cloud API which a server can't reach — so Love is implemented via Last.fm track.love.

nodes.label: Device Discovery (SSDP M-SEARCH + IP-range Scan)
nodes.rationale: SSDP needs UDP multicast (host networking); the /24 IP-range scan probing getStatusEx works in Docker bridge mode.
